task:
Implement the following plan:

# Add Debug Overlays panel to ImGui DebugInspector

## Context
Debug visualization systems (ColliderDebugSystem, SpriteDebugSystem, CullingSystem debug bounds) exist but are toggled by commenting/uncommenting code or flipping static fields. We want runtime toggles via the existing ImGui DebugInspector (F1) so developers can enable/disable debug overlays without recompiling.

## Approach
- Unconditionally add debug systems to the draw pipeline (they default to `Enabled = false`, so zero visual impact until toggled)
- Add a "Debug Overlays" ImGui panel to DebugInspector with checkboxes that flip the static `Enabled` fields
- Make CullingSystem's `DebugEnabled` static (matching the pattern of the other debug systems) so the inspector can toggle it without holding an instance reference

## Changes

### 1. Default `Enabled` to `false` on debug systems
- `MonoDreams/System/Debug/ColliderDebugSystem.cs`: `public static bool Enabled = true;` → `false`
- `MonoDreams/System/Debug/SpriteDebugSystem.cs`: `public static bool Enabled = true;` → `false`

### 2. Make CullingSystem.DebugEnabled static
`MonoDreams/System/Draw/CullingSystem.cs`: Change `public bool DebugEnabled { get; set; } = false;` to `public static bool DebugEnabled = false;`
(Only one CullingSystem instance exists per screen — no multi-instance concern)

### 3. Uncomment debug systems in LoadLevelExampleGameScreen
`MonoDreams.Examples/Screens/LoadLevelExampleGameScreen.cs` — in `CreateDrawSystem()`, uncomment and add after MeshPrepSystem:
```csharp
new MeshPrepSystem(_world),
new ColliderDebugSystem(_world),
new SpriteDebugSystem(_world)
```
They're always in the pipeline but dormant until toggled via inspector.

### 4. Add Debug Overlays panel to DebugInspector
`MonoDreams.Examples/Inspector/DebugInspector.cs`:
- Add `using MonoDreams.System.Debug;` and `using MonoDreams.System.Draw;`
- Add `DrawDebugOverlaysPanel()` called from `Draw()` after existing panels
- Three checkboxes fli
